---
title: "Longitudinal Dataset Creation"
output: html_document
---

# Longitudinal test for CFAR Social Networks

Check-ins are at 6/12/18/24mo periods

### Preliminaries 

```{r echo = T, results = "hide", message = F, warnings = F}
library(tidyr) # data cleaning processing 
library(dplyr) # further data manipulation (%>% (pipe) operator)
library(igraph) # social networks 
library(RColorBrewer) # color palettes 
library(stringr) # regex 
library(purrr) # used to create the network objects 
```


### Data Preparation

We want to be able to stratify by age at enrollment, HEU/HUU, and both together 

- We will then repeat analysis (metrics) at eat time component 
- But first, we will focus on visualization 
- Already have proven that the set.seed() function allows the algorithm to deterministically keep the clusters in the same positions in Igraph plots per-timepoint... Though there is no algorithmic guarantee that these will stay the same across timepoints
- The cleanest way to do this is just to create new datasets at each timepoint and plot from those... to avoid double counting

```{r}
raw_data <- read.csv("/Users/maxschultz/CFAR-SN-Project/Datasets/CHERISHSN_DATA_2026-03-24_1747(in).csv")
#head(raw_data)
```

treat each arm as its own timepoint, delivery_arm_3 is enrolled at delivery and the prefix for the rest delineates the chek-in time. cfar_arm_1 are people who were enrolled in the cross-sectional portion only (n=15) but if they have a csn_k_id then they are part of the longitudinal analysis and timepoint is cfar_v_timepoint_c - information is later under their k ids 


delete all cfars lol -- This was the final determination in the last meeting with Dr. Campbell

15 in total deleting from the longitudinal - this was actually determined to be 30 

```{r}
# number of cfars deleting from the data
length(unique(raw_data$record_id[which(raw_data$redcap_event_name == "cfar_arm_1")]))
```

So there are actually 30 unique index patients who are being 'removed' from the longitudinal analysis.

Here is the code for just the contact-level data population for the longitudinal analysis. 

### Contact Longitudinal Dataset Creation 

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(purrr)


# this is the raw long data without cfar_arm_1 and created timepoints
base_long <- raw_data %>%
  filter(redcap_event_name != "cfar_arm_1") %>%
  mutate(
    record_id = as.character(record_id),
    timepoint = case_when(
      redcap_event_name == "delivery_arm_3" ~ "0m",
      redcap_event_name == "6_month_arm_3" ~ "6m",
      redcap_event_name == "12_month_arm_3" ~ "12m",
      redcap_event_name == "12_month_k23c2_arm_4" ~ "12m",
      redcap_event_name == "18_month_k23c2_arm_4" ~ "18m",
      redcap_event_name == "24_month_k23c2_arm_4" ~ "24m",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(timepoint))



# Contact variables are stored as:
# 0m:  snml_sex_1, snml_vage_1, snml_relate_1, hh_id_1
# 6m:  snml_sex_1_6m, snml_vage_1_6m, snml_relate_1_6m, hh_id_1_6m
# 12m: snml_sex_1_12m, etc.
# 18m: snml_sex_1_18m, etc.
# 24m: snml_sex_1_24m, etc.

# helper function to deal with the regex 
make_basic_contacts <- function(data, tp, suffix_pattern, names_pattern) {
  data %>%
    filter(timepoint == tp) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = matches(suffix_pattern),
      names_to = c(".value", "ctc_idx"),
      names_pattern = names_pattern,
      values_drop_na = FALSE
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      record_id = as.character(record_id),
      ctc_idx = as.integer(ctc_idx),
      timepoint = as.character(tp),
      contact_visit_id = str_c("ctc_", ctc_idx, "_tp_", timepoint, "_idx_", record_id)
    ) %>%
    filter(
      !is.na(snml_sex) |
        !is.na(snml_vage) |
        !is.na(snml_relate) |
        !is.na(hh_id)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      record_id,
      ctc_idx,
      contact_visit_id,
      timepoint,

      snml_vage_mrg = as.numeric(snml_vage),
      snml_sex_mrg = factor(snml_sex),
      snml_relate_mrg = factor(snml_relate),
      hh_id_unified_ = factor(hh_id),

      pcare_mrg = if ("pcare" %in% names(.)) factor(pcare) else factor(NA),
      smoke_mrg = if ("smoke" %in% names(.)) factor(smoke) else factor(NA),
      drug_mrg = if ("drug" %in% names(.)) factor(drug) else factor(NA),
      tb_dx_mrg = if ("tb_dx" %in% names(.)) factor(tb_dx) else factor(NA),
      room_mrg = if ("room" %in% names(.)) factor(room) else factor(NA),
      bed_mrg = if ("bed" %in% names(.)) factor(bed) else factor(NA)
    )
}


# this makes the entire dataset without the exposure variables like lived, stayed, occas., etc. 
basic_contacts <- bind_rows(
  make_basic_contacts(
    base_long,
    tp = "0m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_\\d+$",
    names_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_(\\d+)$"
  ),
  make_basic_contacts(
    base_long,
    tp = "6m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_\\d+_6m$",
    names_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_(\\d+)_6m$"
  ),
  make_basic_contacts(
    base_long,
    tp = "12m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_\\d+_12m$",
    names_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_(\\d+)_12m$"
  ),
  make_basic_contacts(
    base_long,
    tp = "18m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_\\d+_18m$",
    names_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_(\\d+)_18m$"
  ),
  make_basic_contacts(
    base_long,
    tp = "24m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_\\d+_24m$",
    names_pattern = "^(snml_sex|snml_vage|snml_relate|hh_id|pcare|tb_dx|smoke|drug|room|bed)_(\\d+)_24m$"
  )
)



# These are checkbox variables stored as:
# 0m:  lived_1___1
# 6m:  lived_1_6m___1
# 12m: lived_1_12m___1
# 18m: lived_1_18m___1
# 24m: lived_1_24m___1

# helper function for exposures 
make_exposure <- function(data, tp, suffix_pattern, names_pattern) {
  data %>%
    filter(timepoint == tp) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = matches(suffix_pattern),
      names_to = c(".value", "ctc_idx"),
      names_pattern = names_pattern,
      values_drop_na = FALSE
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      record_id = as.character(record_id),
      ctc_idx = as.integer(ctc_idx),
      timepoint = as.character(tp)
    ) %>%
    transmute(
      record_id,
      ctc_idx,
      timepoint,
      lived_mrg = as.integer(lived),
      stayed_mrg = as.integer(stayed),
      visited_mrg = as.integer(visited),
      occas_mrg = as.integer(occas),
      elswe_mrg = as.integer(elswe)
    )
}


# -----------------------------
# 5. Exposure contact variables
# -----------------------------
# exposure_contacts is a reshaped lookup table.
# It includes all possible REDCap contact slots, so do not interpret it
# as the number of real contacts.

exposure_contacts <- bind_rows(
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "0m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)___1$"
  ),
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "6m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+_6m___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)_6m___1$"
  ),
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "12m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+_12m___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)_12m___1$"
  ),
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "18m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+_18m___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)_18m___1$"
  ),
  make_exposure(
    base_long,
    tp = "24m",
    suffix_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_\\d+_24m___1$",
    names_pattern = "^(lived|stayed|visited|occas|elswe)_(\\d+)_24m___1$"
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    record_id = as.character(record_id),
    ctc_idx = as.integer(ctc_idx),
    timepoint = as.character(timepoint)
  )



# creating the final longitudinal dataset 
df_contacts <- basic_contacts %>%
  left_join(
    exposure_contacts,
    by = c("record_id", "ctc_idx", "timepoint")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    timepoint = factor(timepoint, levels = c("0m", "6m", "12m", "18m", "24m"))
  ) %>%
  select(
    record_id,
    contact_visit_id,
    timepoint,

    snml_vage_mrg,
    snml_sex_mrg,
    snml_relate_mrg,
    hh_id_unified_,

    pcare_mrg,
    smoke_mrg,
    drug_mrg,
    tb_dx_mrg,

    lived_mrg,
    stayed_mrg,
    visited_mrg,
    occas_mrg,
    elswe_mrg,

    room_mrg,
    bed_mrg
  )



# some benchmarking checks 
df_contacts %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_index_cases = n_distinct(record_id),
    missing_contact_age = sum(is.na(snml_vage_mrg)),
    missing_contact_sex = sum(is.na(snml_sex_mrg)),
    missing_contact_relationship = sum(is.na(snml_relate_mrg)),
    missing_household_id = sum(is.na(hh_id_unified_)),
    missing_lived = sum(is.na(lived_mrg)),
    missing_stayed = sum(is.na(stayed_mrg)),
    missing_visited = sum(is.na(visited_mrg)),
    missing_occas = sum(is.na(occas_mrg)),
    missing_elswe = sum(is.na(elswe_mrg))
  )

base_long %>%
  count(timepoint)

basic_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, lived_mrg)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, stayed_mrg)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, visited_mrg)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, occas_mrg)

df_contacts %>%
  count(timepoint, elswe_mrg)

head(df_contacts, 20)
```


```{r}
length(unique(raw_data$record_id)) # full raw data 
length(unique(base_long$record_id)) # raw long data without cfar_arm_1
length(unique(basic_contacts$record_id)) # pivoted contact variables where at least one of the variables (lived, stayed, visited, occas., etc. are present) -> so there are technically only 30 usable index cases??
length(unique(df_contacts$record_id))
```


### Index Longitudinal Dataset Creation 

```{r}
raw_data %>%
  group_by(redcap_event_name) %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    enrol_age_nonmissing = sum(!is.na(enrol_age)),
    study_group_nonmissing = sum(!is.na(cherish_sn_study_group)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```


Okay, so this confirms that index-level attributes are only contained within the cfar_arm_1 event so we cant merge them... ignoring this for now. 

```{r}
index_data <- raw_data[which(raw_data$redcap_event_name == "cfar_arm_1"),]
setdiff(unique(index_data$record_id), unique(df_contacts$record_id)) 
```



### Pre-plot Metric Analysis

```{r}
```


### Plotting 

```{r}
# Make sure columns are properly typed
df_contacts <- df_contacts %>%
  mutate(
    record_id = as.character(record_id),
    contact_visit_id = as.character(contact_visit_id),
    timepoint = factor(as.character(timepoint), levels = c("0m", "6m", "12m", "18m", "24m")),
    hh_id_unified_ = as.character(hh_id_unified_)
  )


network_timepoint <- function(data, type = c("basic", "intra_household")) {
  type <- match.arg(type)
  
  # Basic ego-contact edges
  ego_edges <- data %>%
    transmute(
      from = as.character(record_id),
      to = as.character(contact_visit_id)
    ) %>%
    filter(
      !is.na(from),
      !is.na(to),
      from != "",
      to != "",
      from != to
    )
  
  if (nrow(ego_edges) == 0) {
    stop("No ego-contact edges were created. Check record_id/contact_visit_id.")
  }
  
  if (type == "basic") {
    edges <- ego_edges
  }
  
  if (type == "intra_household") {
    hh_edges <- data %>%
      filter(
        !is.na(hh_id_unified_),
        hh_id_unified_ != "",
        hh_id_unified_ != "NA",
        !is.na(contact_visit_id),
        contact_visit_id != ""
      ) %>%
      group_by(record_id, hh_id_unified_) %>%
      summarise(
        pairs = list({
          u <- unique(contact_visit_id)
          
          if (length(u) < 2) {
            tibble::tibble(from = character(0), to = character(0))
          } else {
            m <- t(combn(u, 2))
            tibble::tibble(from = m[, 1], to = m[, 2])
          }
        }),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      tidyr::unnest(pairs)
    
    edges <- bind_rows(ego_edges, hh_edges) %>%
      distinct()
  }
  
  # Make undirected edges unique
  edges <- edges %>%
    mutate(
      a = pmin(from, to),
      b = pmax(from, to)
    ) %>%
    distinct(a, b) %>%
    transmute(from = a, to = b)
  
  graph <- graph_from_data_frame(edges, directed = FALSE)
  
  egos <- unique(as.character(data$record_id))
  
  V(graph)$type <- ifelse(V(graph)$name %in% egos, "index", "contact")
  V(graph)$size <- ifelse(V(graph)$type == "index", 5, 3)
  
  graph
}


# Basic index/contact colors
apply_basic_colors <- function(graph) {
  V(graph)$plot_color <- ifelse(
    V(graph)$type == "index",
    "red",
    "lightblue"
  )

  graph
}


# Contact age colors
contact_age_labels <- c(
  "0-2y", "3-5y", "7-11y", "12-15y", "16-20y",
  "21-30y", "31-40y", "41-50y", "51-60y", ">60y"
)

contact_age_palette <- setNames(
  RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(10, "Spectral"),
  contact_age_labels
)

apply_contact_age_colors <- function(graph, data) {
  
  graph <- apply_basic_colors(graph)
  
  age_lookup <- data %>%
    mutate(
      contact_visit_id = as.character(contact_visit_id),
      snml_vage_mrg = as.character(snml_vage_mrg)
    ) %>%
    distinct(contact_visit_id, snml_vage_mrg)
  
  age_vector <- age_lookup$snml_vage_mrg
  names(age_vector) <- age_lookup$contact_visit_id
  
  contacts <- which(V(graph)$type == "contact")
  contact_names <- V(graph)$name[contacts]
  age_values <- age_vector[contact_names]
  
  age_label_lookup <- setNames(contact_age_labels, as.character(1:10))
  age_labels <- age_label_lookup[as.character(age_values)]
  
  V(graph)$plot_color[contacts] <- ifelse(
    is.na(age_labels),
    "white",
    unname(contact_age_palette[age_labels])
  )
  
  V(graph)$plot_color[V(graph)$type == "index"] <- "red"
  
  graph
}


make_hh_groups <- function(graph, data, alpha = 0.25) {
  
  hh_palette <- c(
    "1" = "brown1",
    "2" = "chocolate1",
    "3" = "yellow",
    "4" = "aquamarine3",
    "5" = "cornflowerblue",
    "6" = "darkorchid2",
    "7" = "deeppink3",
    "NA" = rgb(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, alpha)
  )
  
  hh_lookup <- data %>%
    mutate(
      contact_visit_id = as.character(contact_visit_id),
      hh_id_unified_ = as.character(hh_id_unified_)
    ) %>%
    distinct(contact_visit_id, hh_id_unified_) %>%
    filter(!is.na(hh_id_unified_), hh_id_unified_ != "")
  
  hh_groups_df <- hh_lookup %>%
    group_by(hh_id_unified_) %>%
    summarise(
      nodes = list(contact_visit_id),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  groups <- lapply(hh_groups_df$nodes, function(x) {
    which(V(graph)$name %in% x)
  })
  
  keep <- lengths(groups) >= 2
  
  groups <- groups[keep]
  hh_ids <- hh_groups_df$hh_id_unified_[keep]
  
  cols <- hh_palette[hh_ids]
  cols[is.na(cols)] <- rgb(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, alpha)
  
  list(
    groups = groups,
    cols = unname(cols),
    labels = paste("Household", hh_ids),
    colors = unname(cols)
  )
}


plot_basic_by_timepoint <- function(data, tp) {
  
  tp_data <- data %>%
    filter(as.character(timepoint) == tp)
  
  if (nrow(tp_data) == 0) {
    message("No data for timepoint: ", tp)
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  set.seed(123)
  
  g <- network_timepoint(tp_data, type = "basic")
  g <- apply_basic_colors(g)
  
  plot.igraph(
    g,
    layout = layout_with_fr(g),
    vertex.color = V(g)$plot_color,
    vertex.size = V(g)$size,
    vertex.label = NA,
    edge.color = "gray70",
    edge.width = 0.5,
    main = paste("Basic Ego Network -", tp)
  )
  
  legend(
    "right",
    legend = c("Index", "Contact"),
    col = c("red", "lightblue"),
    pch = 19,
    bty = "n",
    cex = 0.9
  )
}


plot_basic_by_timepoint(df_contacts, "0m")
plot_basic_by_timepoint(df_contacts, "6m")
plot_basic_by_timepoint(df_contacts, "12m")
plot_basic_by_timepoint(df_contacts, "18m")
plot_basic_by_timepoint(df_contacts, "24m")
```
